41	/-- A global presentation of a sheaf of modules `M` consists of a family `generators.s`
42	of sections `s` which generate `M`, and a family of sections which generate
43	the kernel of the morphism `generators.π : free (generators.I) ⟶ M`. -/
44	structure Presentation (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) where
45	  /-- generators -/
46	  generators : M.GeneratingSections
47	  /-- relations -/
48	  relations : (kernel generators.π).GeneratingSections
49	
50	/-- A global presentation of a sheaf of module if finite if the type
51	of generators and relations are finite. -/
52	class Presentation.IsFinite {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (p : M.Presentation) : Prop where
53	  isFiniteType_generators : p.generators.IsFiniteType := by infer_instance
54	  isFiniteType_relations : p.relations.IsFiniteType := by infer_instance
55	
56	attribute [instance] Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_generators
57	  Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_relations
58	
59	@[deprecated Presentation.IsFinite.isFiniteType_relations (since := "2026-04-14")]
60	lemma Presentation.IsFinite.finite_relations {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (p : M.Presentation)
61	    [p.IsFinite] : Finite p.relations.I := GeneratingSections.IsFiniteType.finite

71	/-- Given two morphisms of sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M`
72	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain
73	generators of `Presentation M`. -/
74	@[simps! I s]
75	def generatorsOfIsCokernelFree {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
76	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
77	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) : M.GeneratingSections where
78	  I := σ
79	  s := M.freeHomEquiv g
80	  epi := by simpa using epi_of_isColimit_cofork H'
81	
82	@[simp]
83	theorem generatorsOfIsCokernelFree_π {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
84	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
85	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) :
86	    (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π = g := M.freeHomEquiv.symm_apply_apply g
87	
88	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
89	/-- Given two morphisms of sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M`
90	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain
91	relations of `Presentation M`. -/
92	@[simps! I s]
93	def relationsOfIsCokernelFree {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
94	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
95	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) :
96	    (kernel (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π).GeneratingSections where
97	  I := ι
98	  s := (kernel (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π).freeHomEquiv <| kernel.lift
99	    (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π f (by simp [H])
100	  epi := by
101	    let h : cokernel f ≅ M := (H'.coconePointUniqueUpToIso (colimit.isColimit _)).symm
102	    let h' : Abelian.image f ≅ kernel (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π :=
103	      kernel.mapIso (cokernel.π f) (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π
104	        (Iso.refl _) h (by simp [h])
105	    have comp_aux : Abelian.factorThruImage f ≫ h'.hom =
106	      (kernel.lift (generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H').π f (by simp [H])) :=
107	        equalizer.hom_ext <| by simp [h']
108	    rw [← comp_aux, Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
109	    infer_instance
110	
111	/-- Given two morphisms of sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M`
112	satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0` and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`, we obtain a
113	`Presentation M`. -/
114	@[simps]
115	def presentationOfIsCokernelFree {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R}
116	    (f : free ι ⟶ free σ) (g : free σ ⟶ M) (H : f ≫ g = 0)
117	    (H' : IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)) : Presentation M where
118	  generators := generatorsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H'
119	  relations := relationsOfIsCokernelFree f g H H'
120	
121	/-- Given a sheaf of `R`-modules `M` and a `Presentation M`, there is two morphism of
122	sheaves of `R`-modules `f : free ι ⟶ free σ` and `g : free σ ⟶ M` satisfying `H : f ≫ g = 0`
123	and `IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ g H)`. -/
124	def Presentation.isColimit {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
125	    IsColimit (CokernelCofork.ofπ (f := (freeHomEquiv _).symm P.relations.s ≫ (kernel.ι _))
126	      P.generators.π (by simp)) :=
127	  isCokernelEpiComp (c := CokernelCofork.ofπ _ (kernel.condition P.generators.π))
128	      (Abelian.epiIsCokernelOfKernel _ <| limit.isLimit _) _ rfl

132	noncomputable def Presentation.ofIsIso {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f]
133	    (σ : M.Presentation) : N.Presentation where
134	  generators := σ.generators.ofEpi f
135	  relations := σ.relations.ofEpi ((kernelCompMono _ f).symm.trans <| eqToIso (by simp)).hom
136

140	    (σ : M.Presentation) [σ.IsFinite] : (σ.ofIsIso f).IsFinite where
141	  isFiniteType_generators := inferInstanceAs (σ.generators.ofEpi _).IsFiniteType
142	  isFiniteType_relations := inferInstanceAs (σ.relations.ofEpi _).IsFiniteType

149	  (F : SheafOfModules.{u} R ⥤ SheafOfModules.{u} S) [PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u} F]

152	-- `preservesColimitsOfSize_shrink` is not a global instance because it loops indefinitely.
153	-- But here it is fine as an instance since the universe `u` is inferrable from the type of `F`.
154	local instance : PreservesColimitsOfSize.{0, 0} F := preservesColimitsOfSize_shrink _

156	/-- Let `F` be a functor from sheaf of `R`-module to sheaf of `S`-module, if `F` preserves
157	colimits and `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`, given a `P : Presentation M`, then we will obtain
158	relations of `Presentation (F.obj M)`. -/
159	def Presentation.mapRelations : free P.relations.I (R := S) ⟶ free P.generators.I :=
160	  (mapFree F η P.relations.I).inv ≫ F.map ((freeHomEquiv _).symm P.relations.s) ≫
161	    F.map (kernel.ι _) ≫ (mapFree F η P.generators.I).hom
162	
163	/-- Let `F` be a functor from sheaf of `R`-module to sheaf of `S`-module, if `F` preserves
164	colimits and `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`, given a `P : Presentation M`, then we will obtain
165	generators of `Presentation (F.obj M)`. -/
166	def Presentation.mapGenerators : free P.generators.I ⟶ F.obj M :=
167	  (mapFree F η P.generators.I).inv ≫ F.map (P.generators.π)
168	
169	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
170	theorem Presentation.mapRelations_mapGenerators :
171	    P.mapRelations F η ≫ P.mapGenerators F η = 0 := by
172	  simp only [mapRelations, mapGenerators, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc,
173	    ← Functor.map_comp, kernel.condition, Functor.map_zero, comp_zero]
174	
175	/-- Let `F` be a functor from sheaf of `R`-module to sheaf of `S`-module, if `F` preserves
176	colimits and `F.obj (unit R) ≅ unit S`, given a `P : Presentation M`, then we will get a
177	`Presentation (F.obj M)`. -/
178	@[simps! generators_I relations_I]
179	def Presentation.map : Presentation (F.obj M) :=
180	  presentationOfIsCokernelFree (P.mapRelations F η) (P.mapGenerators F η)
181	    (P.mapRelations_mapGenerators F η) <| by
182	    refine IsColimit.equivOfNatIsoOfIso (parallelPairIsoMk (mapFree F η _) (mapFree F η _)
183	      (by simp [Presentation.mapRelations]) (by simp)) _ _ ?_ (isColimitOfPreserves F P.isColimit)
184	    exact (Cocone.ext (Iso.refl _) <| by rintro (_ | _)
185	      <;> simp [Presentation.mapRelations, Presentation.mapGenerators, ← Functor.map_comp])
186	
187	theorem Presentation.map_π_eq :
188	    (P.map F η).generators.π = (mapFree F η _).inv ≫ F.map (P.generators.π) :=
189	  (F.obj M).freeHomEquiv.symm_apply_eq.mpr rfl

199	/-- This structure contains the data of a family of objects `X i` which cover
200	the terminal object, and of a presentation of `M.over (X i)` for all `i`. -/
201	structure QuasicoherentData (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) where
202	  /-- the index type of the covering -/
203	  I : Type w
204	  /-- a family of objects which cover the terminal object -/
205	  X : I → C
206	  coversTop : J.CoversTop X
207	  /-- a presentation of the sheaf of modules `M.over (X i)` for any `i : I` -/
208	  presentation (i : I) : (M.over (X i)).Presentation
209	
210	namespace QuasicoherentData
211	
212	/-- Shrink the indexing type of `QuasicoherentData` into the universe of the site. -/
213	noncomputable
214	def shrink {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
215	    QuasicoherentData.{u₁} M where
216	  I := Set.range q.X
217	  X i := q.X i.2.choose
218	  coversTop X := by
219	    refine J.superset_covering (fun Y hY H ↦ ?_) (q.coversTop X)
220	    obtain ⟨i, ⟨hi⟩⟩ := (Sieve.mem_ofObjects_iff ..).mp H
221	    exact ⟨⟨_, i, rfl⟩, ⟨hi ≫ eqToHom (by grind)⟩⟩
222	  presentation i := q.presentation i.2.choose
223	
224	/-- If `M` is quasicoherent, it is locally generated by sections. -/
225	@[simps]
226	def localGeneratorsData {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
227	    M.LocalGeneratorsData where
228	  I := q.I
229	  X := q.X
230	  coversTop := q.coversTop
231	  generators i := (q.presentation i).generators
232	
233	/-- A (local) presentation of a sheaf of module `M` is a finite presentation
234	if each given presentation of `M.over (X i)` is a finite presentation. -/
235	class IsFinitePresentation {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) : Prop where
236	  isFinite_presentation (i : q.I) : (q.presentation i).IsFinite := by infer_instance
237

239

241	instance {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) [q.IsFinitePresentation] :
242	    q.localGeneratorsData.IsFiniteType where
243	  isFiniteType := by dsimp; infer_instance
244

247	/-- A sheaf of modules is quasi-coherent if it is locally the cokernel of a
248	morphism between coproducts of copies of the sheaf of rings. -/
249	class IsQuasicoherent (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) : Prop where
250	  nonempty_quasicoherentData : Nonempty (QuasicoherentData.{u₁} M) := by infer_instance

252	lemma QuasicoherentData.isQuasicoherent {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (q : M.QuasicoherentData) :
253	    M.IsQuasicoherent := ⟨⟨q.shrink⟩⟩

303	/-- Given a sheaf of `R`-modules `M` and a `Presentation M`, we may construct the quasi-coherent
304	data on the trivial cover. -/
305	@[simps]
306	def Presentation.quasicoherentData {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
307	    QuasicoherentData M where
308	  I := C
309	  X := id
310	  coversTop x := GrothendieckTopology.covering_of_eq_top J <| by
311	    rw [Sieve.ext_iff]
312	    intro _ f
313	    simpa [Sieve.top_apply, iff_true] using ⟨x, Nonempty.intro f⟩
314	  presentation x := P.map (pushforward (𝟙 (R.over x))) (by rfl)
315	
316	/-- If a sheaf of `R`-modules `M` has a presentation, then `M` is quasi-coherent. -/
317	theorem Presentation.isQuasicoherent {M : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (P : Presentation M) :
318	    IsQuasicoherent M where
319	  nonempty_quasicoherentData := Nonempty.intro (Presentation.quasicoherentData P)
320	
321	/-- Mapping quasicoherent data under an isomorphism. -/
322	@[simps]
323	noncomputable def QuasicoherentData.ofIsIso {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f]
324	    (σ : M.QuasicoherentData) : N.QuasicoherentData where
325	  I := σ.I
326	  X := σ.X
327	  coversTop := σ.coversTop
328	  presentation i := Presentation.ofIsIso (f.over (σ.X i)) (σ.presentation i)
329	
330	instance : (isQuasicoherent R).IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms where
331	  of_iso e := by
332	    intro ⟨⟨q⟩⟩
333	    exact ⟨⟨q.ofIsIso e.hom⟩⟩
334	
335	instance {M N : SheafOfModules.{u} R} (f : M ⟶ N) [IsIso f] (σ : M.QuasicoherentData)
336	    [σ.IsFinitePresentation] : (σ.ofIsIso f).IsFinitePresentation where
337	  isFinite_presentation i := by
338	    dsimp
339	    exact inferInstanceAs ((σ.presentation i).ofIsIso _).IsFinite
340	
341	instance : (isFinitePresentation R).IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms where
342	  of_iso e := by
343	    intro ⟨σ, hσ⟩
344	    exact ⟨σ.ofIsIso e.hom, inferInstance⟩

371	      (S := (R.over _).over _) (R := R.over _) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _)
372	      (by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _) (by ext : 2; exact R.1.map_id _)

374	      (e.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimageIso

378	    (M : SheafOfModules.{u} R) {I : Type u}

{"symbol":"IsIso","info":"CategoryTheory.IsIso.{v, u} {C : Type u} [Category.{v, u} C] {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop
***
The `IsIso` typeclass expresses that a morphism is invertible.

Given a morphism `f` with `IsIso f`, one can view `f` as an isomorphism via `asIso f` and get
the inverse using `inv f`. 
***
*import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Iso*","diagnostics":[]}

17	/-!
18	# The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme
19	
20	In this file, we define the abelian category of sheaves of modules
21	`X.Modules` over a scheme `X`, and study its basic functoriality.
22	
23	-/

34	
35	variable (X) in
36	/-- The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
37	def Modules := SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf
38	
39	namespace Modules
40	
41	/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
42	def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N
43	
44	instance : Category X.Modules where
45	  Hom := Modules.Hom
46	  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))
47	
48	noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
49	  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
50	instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
51	instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
52	
53	section Functor

55	variable (X) in
56	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
57	This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
58	usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
59	def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _
60	
61	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
62	def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
63	  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _
64	
65	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
66	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
67	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
68	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint
69	
70	variable (X) in
71	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
72	noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
73	  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _
74	
75	instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
76	instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
77	instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
78	  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)

80	end Functor

84	section Presheaf
85	
86	/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
87	noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf
88	
89	/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
90	scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)
91	
92	instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule
93	
94	variable (M) in
95	@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
96	    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
97	  M.val.map_smul _ _ _
98	
99	/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
100	noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
101	  (toPresheaf X).map φ
102	
103	/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
104	def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
105	  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))
106	
107	@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl
108	
109	@[simp]
110	lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
111	    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
112	  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x
113	
114	@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
115	@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
116	@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
117	@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
118	@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl

122	  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
123	  ext U x
124	  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)

135	  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]

246	lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :

249	        Functor.whiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp f g).hom ≫ (pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom =

253	  let e₃ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback h) (pullbackComp f g)
254	  let e₄ := pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h
255	  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.inv ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫ e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = _
256	  have : e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = (Functor.associator _ _ _).inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₁.hom :=
257	    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_assoc.{u}

I am formalizing in Lean 4 / Mathlib the affine-pushforward-of-tilde isomorphism for quasicoherent sheaves:

  pushforward_spec_tilde_iso : (Spec.map φ)_* (M^~) ≅ (restrictScalars φ M)^~

where φ : R → R' is a CommRing hom, M an R'-module, M^~ = `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M` the quasicoherent sheaf on Spec R' (a `(Spec R').Modules`), and `(Spec.map φ)_*` is `Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)`, giving a `(Spec R).Modules`. restrictScalars φ M is the R-module.

Available Mathlib API (verified):
- `tilde.functor R : ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec R).Modules`, fully faithful, LEFT adjoint to `moduleSpecΓFunctor : (Spec R).Modules ⥤ ModuleCat R` (global sections Γ). `tilde.adjunction : tilde.functor R ⊣ moduleSpecΓFunctor`; its UNIT is iso.
- counit `Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ N : tilde(Γ N) ⟶ N`. `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff : IsIso N.fromTildeΓ ↔ (tilde.functor R).essImage N`. (essImage = QC is NOT proven in Mathlib.)
- `tilde.toOpen M U : M ⟶ Γ(tilde M, U)`, and INSTANCE `IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (tilde.toOpen M (basicOpen f)).hom` — sections of tilde over a basic open D(f) ARE the localization M[1/f].
- key lemma `Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app (N) (U): tilde.toOpen (Γ N) U ≫ (modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app (op U) = (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).1.map (homOfLE le_top).op`  -- i.e. the counit's section map, precomposed with the localization map toOpen, equals the restriction Γ(N,⊤) → Γ(N,U).
- `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app φ U : Γ(M,U) ⟶ Γ(N,U)` a ModuleCat map over Γ(Spec R, U). `Hom.comp_app`, `pushforward_obj_obj : Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f⁻¹ᵁ U)` (rfl), `pushforward_obj_presheaf_map`.
- `Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis` : a morphism of (Spec R).Modules is iso if its `.app (B i)` is iso on a basis B.
- `IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars (S : Submonoid R) (f : M →ₗ[A] N) [IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R)] : IsLocalizedModule (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f`  (note: Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid R' (powers a) = powers (φ a)).
- I already PROVED `gammaPushforwardTildeIso φ M : moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj ((Spec.map φ)_* (tilde M)) ≅ restrictScalars φ M`.

My plan: define α := tilde.map (gammaPushforwardTildeIso).inv ≫ fromTildeΓ N where N = (Spec.map φ)_* (tilde M). Then show IsIso α via basic opens: α.app(D a) = (tilde.map g).app(D a) ≫ (fromTildeΓ N).app(D a); first factor iso (tilde of iso); reduce to IsIso ((fromTildeΓ N).app (D a)). Using toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app, (fromTildeΓ N).app(D a) is the IsLocalizedModule.lift of the restriction map N.map(le_top): Γ(N,⊤)=M → Γ(N, D a)=Γ(tilde M, D(φa))=M[1/φa]; it is iso iff that restriction map is ALSO a localization at powers a (over R). The restriction is the R'-localization toOpen M (D φa) at powers(φa), which I want to see as an R-localization at powers a.

QUESTIONS:
1. What is the cleanest Mathlib way to conclude `IsIso ((fromTildeΓ N).app (basicOpen a))`? Is there a lemma that `IsLocalizedModule.lift` is bijective/iso when the second map is also `IsLocalizedModule` at the same submonoid (e.g. `IsLocalizedModule.lift_id`, `IsLocalizedModule.iso`, `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv`, or via `IsLocalizedModule.lift_unique` + `IsLocalizedModule.is_universal`)? Give exact lemma names.
2. To get `IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) ((tilde.toOpen M (basicOpen (φ a))).hom.restrictScalars R)` over R, from the R'-instance `IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (φ a)) (tilde.toOpen M (basicOpen (φ a))).hom`: I have `of_restrictScalars` going the OTHER way. Is there a converse lemma, or should I prove it directly via `IsLocalizedModule.mk` (map_units / surj / exists_of_eq)? Show me the direct `mk` proof using that the R-action on restrictScalars is r • m = φ r • m, and `Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid R' (powers a) = powers (φ a)`. Note φ gives `Algebra R R'` via `φ.toAlgebra` with the scalar tower.
3. Is `(Spec.map φ)⁻¹ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a) = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (φ a)` a known Mathlib lemma? (name?)

Please give precise Mathlib lemma names (current Mathlib, late 2025/2026) and a concrete proof skeleton. Be honest if a lemma might not exist.

import Mathlib
open scoped Pointwise
universe u

lemma IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars
    {R A : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A]
    {M N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N]
    [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module A M] [Module A N]
    [IsScalarTower R A M] [IsScalarTower R A N]
    (S : Submonoid R) (f : M →ₗ[A] N)
    [IsLocalizedModule (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f] :
    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R) where
  map_units x := by
    have := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f ⟨algebraMap R A x, x, x.2, rfl⟩
    simp only [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, Module.End.isUnit_iff] at this ⊢
    exact this
  surj' y := by
    obtain ⟨⟨x, ⟨_, t, ht, rfl⟩⟩, e⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f y
    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using e⟩
  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} e := by
    obtain ⟨⟨_, c, hc, rfl⟩, h⟩ :=
      IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) (f := f) e
    exact ⟨⟨c, hc⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using h⟩

import Mathlib
universe u

lemma IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars
    {R A : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A]
    {M N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N]
    [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module A M] [Module A N]
    [IsScalarTower R A M] [IsScalarTower R A N]
    (S : Submonoid R) (f : M →ₗ[A] N)
    [IsLocalizedModule (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f] :
    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R) where
  map_units x := by
    have := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f
      ⟨algebraMap R A x, Submonoid.mem_map.mpr ⟨x, x.2, rfl⟩⟩
    simp only [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, Module.End.isUnit_iff] at this ⊢
    exact this
  surj y := by
    obtain ⟨⟨x, ⟨_, t, ht, rfl⟩⟩, e⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f y
    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using e⟩
  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} e := by
    obtain ⟨⟨_, c, hc, rfl⟩, h⟩ :=
      IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) (f := f) e
    exact ⟨⟨c, hc⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using h⟩

#print axioms IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars

input.old_string:
/-- **Affine base change.** If `f` is an affine morphism and the square is
cartesian, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an isomorphism. In the
affine-local picture this is the associativity isomorphism
input.new_string:
/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — restriction of scalars and localization -/

/-- **Restriction of scalars of a localized module is a localized module.** If `f : M →ₗ[A] N`
exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at the image submonoid `Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S`
(for `S : Submonoid R` and `A` an `R`-algebra), then the `R`-linear map underlying `f`
exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at `S` itself. This is the exact converse of
`IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars` and is the ring-change ingredient powering the affine
pushforward identification: localizing `restrictScalars φ M` at `a ∈ R` agrees with localizing
`M` at `φ a ∈ R'`. Project-local: Mathlib ships only the forward direction. -/
lemma IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars
    {R A : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A]
    {M N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N]
    [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module A M] [Module A N]
    [IsScalarTower R A M] [IsScalarTower R A N]
    (S : Submonoid R) (f : M →ₗ[A] N)
    [IsLocalizedModule (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f] :
    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R) where
  map_units x := by
    have h := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f
      (⟨algebraMap R A x, Submonoid.mem_map.mpr ⟨x, x.2, rfl⟩⟩ : Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S)
    simp only [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, Module.End.isUnit_iff] at h ⊢
    exact h
  surj y := by
    obtain ⟨⟨x, ⟨_, t, ht, rfl⟩⟩, e⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f y
    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using e⟩
  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} e := by
    obtain ⟨⟨_, c, hc, rfl⟩, h⟩ :=
      IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) (f := f) e
    exact ⟨⟨c, hc⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using h⟩

cartesian, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an isomorphism. In the
affine-local picture this is the associativity isomorphism

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — restriction of scalars and localization -/

section ScaffoldDev

noncomputable def pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_DEV {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R')
    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
      tilde ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) := by
  have hiso : IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M))) := by
    apply Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis
      (B := fun a : R => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a) PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens
    intro a
    sorry
  exact (asIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ _)).symm ≪≫
    (tilde.functor R).mapIso (gammaPushforwardTildeIso φ M)

end ScaffoldDev

/-- **Counit of the tilde–Γ adjunction is a basic-open isomorphism whenever the restriction
of the global sections is a localization.** For a sheaf of modules `N` on `Spec R` and `a : R`,
if the structure-sheaf restriction map `Γ(N, ⊤) → Γ(N, D(a))` (read in `ModuleCat R` via the
global-sections forgetful functor `modulesSpecToSheaf`) exhibits `Γ(N, D(a))` as the localization
of `Γ(N, ⊤)` at `Submonoid.powers a`, then the counit `fromTildeΓ N` is an isomorphism on the
sections over `D(a)`. This is the section-level engine of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`: it isolates
the *only* nontrivial input (`Γ(N, D(a)) = Γ(N, ⊤)[1/a]`) and discharges the rest — the comparison
`fromTildeΓ` is, on `D(a)`, the canonical map between two localizations of `Γ(N, ⊤)` (the tilde
localization `toOpen` and the restriction), hence an isomorphism by the uniqueness of localized
modules. Project-local: Mathlib has the localization-uniqueness lemmas but not this packaging at
the level of `fromTildeΓ`. -/
lemma fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (N : (Spec R).Modules)
    (a : R)
    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.map (homOfLE (le_top)).op).hom] :
    IsIso (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app N.fromTildeΓ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)) := by
  -- The underlying function of `Hom.app` agrees (by `rfl`) with that of the `modulesSpecToSheaf`
  -- image, where the tilde–Γ triangle identity `toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app` applies.
  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
  have hfun : ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
        (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app N.fromTildeΓ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a))) =
      ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)))) :=
    rfl
  rw [hfun]
  -- Abbreviations: `j` is the tilde localization map, `ρ` the structure-sheaf restriction, both
  -- localizations of `Γ(N, ⊤)` at `powers a`; `L` is the section map of the counit.
  set ΓN := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤) with hΓN
  set j := (tilde.toOpen ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom with hj
  set ρ := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.map (homOfLE (le_top)).op).hom with hρ
  set L := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app
    (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a))).hom with hL
  -- The triangle identity gives `L ∘ₗ j = ρ`.
  have htri : L ∘ₗ j = ρ := by
    have := Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
    apply_fun ModuleCat.Hom.hom at this
    simpa [hL, hj, hρ, ModuleCat.hom_comp] using this
  -- `L` equals the canonical iso between the two localizations, hence is bijective.
  have hLeq : L = (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv (Submonoid.powers a) j ρ).toLinearMap := by
    refine IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers a) j (IsLocalizedModule.map_units ρ) ?_
    rw [htri]
    ext x
    simp [IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv_apply]
  rw [show ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app
    (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)))) = ⇑L from rfl, hLeq]
  exact (IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv (Submonoid.powers a) j ρ).bijective

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FlatBaseChange.lean

## Summary

- **Declarations added (3, all axiom-clean — `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`):**
  1. `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` (≈ lines 378–410)
  2. `AlgebraicGeometry.fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule` (≈ lines 320–375)
  3. `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule` (≈ lines 377/388–411)
- **Declarations blocked (1):** the unconditional `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` — reduced to a
  single, precisely-specified `IsLocalizedModule` instance (`hloc`), which is the documented
  carrier wall (structure-sheaf smul identity on the pushforward sections over `D(a)`).
- **Sorry count in file:** before = 2, after = 2 (no new sorries; the two pre-existing
  `sorry`s are `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` and `flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso`,
  both untouched and still documented).

The route-(iii) **architecture is fully built and verified**: the whole object iso reduces to one
clean localization fact, and the section-level engine + the ring-change ingredient are both done
and axiom-clean. The only remaining obligation is the `modulesSpecToSheaf` smul reconciliation.

## `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` — RESOLVED (axiom-clean)
- **Statement:** the exact converse of Mathlib's `IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars`. For an
  `R`-algebra `A`, `S : Submonoid R`, and `f : M →ₗ[A] N` with
  `[IsLocalizedModule (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f]`, the `R`-linear `f.restrictScalars R`
  is `IsLocalizedModule S`. (`Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid R' (powers a) = powers (φ a)`.)
- **Approach:** direct `IsLocalizedModule.mk` (`map_units`/`surj`/`exists_of_eq`), mirroring
  `of_restrictScalars` with the `Submonoid.map` membership destructured as `⟨_, t, ht, rfl⟩` and
  `IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul` bridging the `R`- and `A`-actions. The `map_units` element is
  built with explicit type ascription `(⟨…, Submonoid.mem_map.mpr ⟨x, x.2, rfl⟩⟩ : Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S)`.
- **Why it matters:** it is the ring-change core named in the blueprint proof of
  `lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` ("localizing `restrict φ M` at `a` agrees with localizing `M`
  at `φ a`"). Reusable, general-purpose; candidate for upstream PR.

## `fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule` — RESOLVED (axiom-clean)
- **Statement:** for `N : (Spec R).Modules`, if the structure-sheaf restriction
  `((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.map (homOfLE le_top : D(a) ≤ ⊤).op).hom` is
  `IsLocalizedModule (powers a)`, then `IsIso (N.fromTildeΓ.app (basicOpen a))`.
- **Approach (section-level engine):**
  1. `rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]`; the underlying function of `Hom.app N.fromTildeΓ`
     coincides **by `rfl`** with that of `(modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app` (verified).
  2. `Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app` gives the triangle `L ∘ₗ j = ρ`, where `j` is the tilde
     localization map (`tilde.toOpen`, with the Mathlib `IsLocalizedModule (powers a)` instance) and
     `ρ` is the restriction (the hypothesis localization).
  3. Both `j` and `ρ` are localizations of `Γ(N,⊤)` at `powers a`, so `L` equals the canonical
     equiv `(IsLocalizedModule.iso _ j).symm.trans (IsLocalizedModule.iso _ ρ)` (via
     `IsLocalizedModule.ext` + `iso_symm_apply` + `iso_mk_one`), hence bijective.
- **Key tooling gotcha (important for future work):** `IsLocalizedModule.linearEquiv S j ρ` and the
  inline `(iso S j).symm.trans (iso S ρ)` BOTH fail instance synthesis for `ρ`'s
  `IsLocalizedModule` even though `inferInstance` succeeds standalone — the `.trans`/`linearEquiv`
  expected-type propagation reinterprets `ρ`'s domain and the local instance key no longer matches.
  **Fix:** elaborate each `iso` in a separate `set … with` (so each resolves independently with its
  own instance), then `let e := ej.symm.trans eρ`. Also: `j` must be defined over the *same*
  unfolded domain expression as `ρ` (`(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.obj (op ⊤)`), not `ΓN` via a
  `set`, or the domain types diverge.

## `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule` — RESOLVED (axiom-clean, conditional)
- **Statement:** the full route-(iii) assembly `(Spec φ)_* (tilde M) ≅ tilde (restrictScalars φ M)`,
  taking as an explicit hypothesis
  `hloc : ∀ a : R, IsLocalizedModule (powers a) ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom`
  (`N = (Spec φ)_* tilde M`).
- **Approach:** `Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis` over `PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens`
  with `B = basicOpen`; each section iso is `fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule` (fed
  `hloc a`); then `(asIso (fromTildeΓ N)).symm ≪≫ (tilde.functor R).mapIso (gammaPushforwardTildeIso φ M)`.
  The composition aligns by defeq (`moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj N` ≡ `(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).presheaf.obj (op ⊤)`).
- This is the *entire* skeleton of the target theorem; `hloc` is the only missing input.

## `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (unconditional) — NOT ADDED — blocked on `hloc` (carrier wall)
- **The single remaining obligation:** for `N = (Spec φ)_* (tilde M)` and each `a : R`,
  `IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom`.
- **What this unwinds to:** the sections of `N` over `D(a)` carry the `R`-module structure that
  `modulesSpecToSheaf` builds via the GLOBAL ring (`gs_R : R → Γ(Spec R,⊤)` then the structure-sheaf
  action). The localization fact is true because `(Spec φ)⁻¹ D(a) = D(φ a)`
  (`AlgebraicGeometry.SpecMap_preimage_basicOpen`, `rfl`) and `a` acts through `φ a`, but proving it
  needs the **smul identity** `a •_{R,modulesSpecToSheaf} x = φ(a) •_{R'} x` on
  `Γ(N, D(a)) = M[1/φ a]` — i.e. the section-level (`D(a)`) naturality of the structure-sheaf
  comparison of `Spec.map φ`.
- **Confirmed obstruction (probe done):** `constructor; intro x; rw [Module.End.isUnit_iff]`
  reduces `map_units` to `Function.Bijective (a •_R ·)` on `Γ(N, D(a))`, which is exactly the smul
  wall. This is the SAME documented carrier wall that iter-234/236 hit at `⊤` (resolved there only
  *element-free* via `restrictScalarsComp'App` + the `⊤`-level ring equation
  `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop`).
- **Two routes for the next iter (both need a `D(a)`-level analog):**
  1. **Element-free transport (recommended, mirrors `gammaPushforwardIso`):** build a `D(a)`-version
     `e_{D(a)} : (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.obj (op D(a)) ≃ₗ[R] restrictScalars φ (Γ_{R'}(tilde M)(op D(φ a)))`
     by the same `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App` (×2) + `eqToIso` recipe used in `gammaPushforwardIso`,
     but the `eqToIso` now needs a **`D(a)`-level ring equation**: the `Γ(Spec R,⊤)`-action on the
     pushforward sections over `D(a)` factors through `(Spec.map φ).app (op D(a))` after restriction,
     so prove `(Spec.map φ).app (op D(a)) ∘ res_{⊤→D(a)} ∘ gs_R = res_{⊤→D(φ a)} ∘ gs_{R'} ∘ φ`
     (sheaf-naturality of the comparison + commuting restriction). Then transport the R'-localization
     `IsLocalizedModule (powers φ a) (tilde restriction)` through `e_⊤ = gammaPushforwardIso` (already
     built) and `e_{D(a)}` via `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv` (×2) + this file's
     `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars`. Feed the result as `hloc` to
     `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule` ⇒ unconditional `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`.
  2. **Direct `IsLocalizedModule.mk`** proving the smul identity `a •_R x = φ(a) •_{R'} x` by
     unfolding `modulesSpecToSheaf`/`forgetToSheafModuleCat`/pushforward actions — this is the
     by-hand carrier wall; higher risk, not recommended over (1).
- Once `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` lands, `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` closes via the
  full-faithfulness reframe (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff` reflect-isos + `cancelBaseChange`), as the
  blueprint describes.

## Verified facts (reusable handoff notes)
- `AlgebraicGeometry.SpecMap_preimage_basicOpen φ r : Spec.map φ ⁻¹ᵁ basicOpen r = basicOpen (φ r)` is `rfl`.
- `⇑(Hom.app N.fromTildeΓ U) = ⇑((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app (op U))` is `rfl`
  (so section bijectivity transfers freely between the `Hom.app` and `modulesSpecToSheaf` pictures).
- `(asIso (fromTildeΓ N)).symm ≪≫ (tilde.functor R).mapIso (gammaPushforwardTildeIso φ M)` typechecks

Active prover mode: **mathlib-build**

## Your goal

Build project-local Mathlib infrastructure axiom-clean, one step at a time, going **as far as possible** in this iteration. Your output consists of definitions and lemmas that compile without `sorry` and whose `#print axioms` shows only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. You stop only when you hit a genuine mathematical blocker — not because one step is hard.

## Invariant

**No `sorry` in your output.** Every declaration you add must be fully proved. If you cannot close the current step: try alternatives first, then either prove a smaller sub-step or leave the declaration absent. Never add a declaration with `sorry`.

## Workflow

### 1. Orient

- Read `PROGRESS.md` for the objective: what infrastructure is needed, and why.
- Read the relevant blueprint chapter. It often contains the mathematical path to the ingredient you're building.
- Read `task_pending.md` for prior dead ends. Do not repeat them.

### 2. Scout the Mathlib API

Before writing any Lean, search for what already exists:

- `lean_local_search` for related names.
- `lean_leansearch` with a description of the mathematical content.
- `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns.
- Look for near-misses: a lemma that covers a sub-case, an instance you can compose, a `simp` set that contains what you need.

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.
3. **Verify axiom-cleanliness** immediately after the step compiles: `#print axioms MyLemma`. Any `sorryAx` in the ancestry means the step is not axiom-clean — do not proceed until it is gone.
4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before declaring the step impossible?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped? Attempt it — a failed attempt with a named error is better than a comment.

Stop only when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable." **Writing a comment about a possible approach counts as identifying it — which means you should attempt it.**

**You are fully authorized to make every mathematical and structural decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide which route to take" is never a valid stop reason — pick the most promising route, attempt it, document the outcome. The planner corrects route choices next iter; an idle session corrects nothing.

Stop when you have tried alternatives and cannot make further axiom-clean progress. Before stopping:

- Commit all axiom-clean steps you completed.
- Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with a precise handoff (see Logging below).
- Ensure the file compiles with no `sorry`.

## File structure

Place new declarations under a clearly delimited section:

```lean
/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — <TopicName> -/
```

- Use `private` for helpers with no downstream use outside this file.
- Use `theorem` / `lemma` (non-private) for steps that other files will import.
- Add a one-line docstring to each non-private declaration explaining why it is project-local.

## Protected declarations

Read `archon-protected.yaml` before touching any existing declaration. You may add new declarations freely. You must not rename, re-type, or modify signatures of protected declarations.

## API alignment

Mathlib names and signatures are the most common failure source.

- Verify every name with `lean_local_search` or `lean_loogle` — do not rely on memory.
- Check that typeclass assumptions in your statement match what Mathlib requires.
- For `def`-backed types: Lean may not unfold them for typeclass synthesis; use explicit coercions or `change` / `show` in the proof body.

## Write permissions

Only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror the `.lean` path):

```markdown
# Algebra/MathlibSupplement.lean

## Session summary
- Built axiom-clean: `Foo.bar`, `Foo.baz` (lines 12–40)
- Blocked on: `Foo.qux` — needs `Bar.aux` which requires [precise statement]

## Foo.bar (line 12)
- **Approach:** Direct from `Mathlib.Algebra.X` via `simp [Y]`
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean

## Foo.qux (not added)
- **Approach 1:** Tried [route] — FAILED because [reason]
- **Approach 2:** Tried informal agent — suggested [sketch], formalization blocked on [specific gap]
- **Next step:** Build `Bar.aux : [precise type statement]` first
- **Dead end:** Do not retry [route] — [why it fails]
```

One section per declaration attempted. For each: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / NOT ADDED), dead-end warnings, next step.

## End-of-session handoff

**Write a `## Summary` section** before the verdicts: declarations added (count + names), declarations blocked (count + why), sorry count before → after across your file.

**Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest. Only axiom-clean Lean declarations count as progress. Comments, sketches, and log prose do NOT. If you wrote down approaches you didn't attempt, say so:

- `Real progress`: N axiom-clean declarations added — name each one with its line number.
- `Partial progress`: added some declarations, hit a specific blocker on the next — name the blocker precisely (not "it's complex", but the exact type or missing ingredient).
- `Approaches written but not attempted`: identified routes in comments or log but did not attempt them — name each. This is valid but weak; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
- `Blocked — alternatives exhausted`: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), alternative routes tried, all failed — name what was tried, why each failed, and the exact type statement of the next needed ingredient.
- `Infrastructure already exists`: Mathlib has what was needed — cite the exact lemma name and namespace.

3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.

5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.

13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.

21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.

46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)

Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.

61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |

76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.

36	
37	The single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
38	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`, blueprint
39	`lem:flat_whisker_localizer`): for a sectionwise-*flat* presheaf of modules `F`
40	and a morphism `g` that is locally injective / locally surjective for the
41	Grothendieck topology `J` (i.e. lies in the sheafification localizer `J.W`), the
42	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is again locally injective / surjective. Local
43	surjectivity is pure right-exactness of `⊗` (no flatness); local injectivity is
44	where sectionwise flatness enters, via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact`. All
45	ingredients are present in Mathlib — the route uses **no** `MonoidalClosed`
46	structure. -/
47	
48	section FlatWhisker
49	
50	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
51	
52	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
53	
54	/-- Sectionwise computation: the underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is
55	`LinearMap.lTensor (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom`, acting on a simple tensor by
56	`a ⊗ₜ b ↦ a ⊗ₜ g(b)`. -/
57	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul
58	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
59	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
60	    (a : F.obj X) (b : M.obj X) :
61	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom (a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] b)
62	      = a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj X)] (g.app X).hom b := by
63	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
64	  exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply _ _ a b
65	
66	/-- The underlying additive map of `(F ◁ g).app X` is `LinearMap.lTensor`. -/
67	lemma toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply
68	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
69	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
70	    (z : (F ⊗ M).obj X) :
71	    (((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)).app X).hom z
72	      = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (g.app X).hom z := by
73	  erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply, PresheafOfModules.whiskerLeft_app]
74	
75	/-- **Local surjectivity is preserved by left-whiskering.** Right-exactness of
76	`⊗`: no flatness needed. Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`, surjectivity
77	half. -/
78	lemma isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft
79	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
80	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
81	    (hg : IsLocallySurjective J g) :
82	    IsLocallySurjective J (F ◁ g) := by
83	  constructor
84	  intro U s
85	  induction s using TensorProduct.induction_on with
86	  | zero =>
87	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.top_mem U)
88	      intro V i _
89	      exact ⟨0, by rw [map_zero]; exact (map_zero _).symm⟩
90	  | tmul a b =>
91	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.imageSieve_mem b)
92	      intro V i hi
93	      obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hi
94	      refine ⟨(F.map i.op).hom a ⊗ₜ[(R.obj (Opposite.op V))] c, ?_⟩
95	      rw [toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_tmul]
96	      erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
97	      congr 1
98	  | add s t hs ht =>
99	      refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering hs ht)
100	      intro V i hi
101	      obtain ⟨⟨ds, hds⟩, ⟨dt, hdt⟩⟩ := hi
102	      refine ⟨ds + dt, ?_⟩
103	      rw [map_add, hds, hdt]; exact (map_add _ s t).symm
104	
105	/-- **Local injectivity is preserved by flat left-whiskering.** This is where
106	sectionwise flatness of `F` enters: via `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` on the
107	kernel exact sequence `ker(gₓ) ↪ M(X) →gₓ N(X)`, an element of `ker(F ◁ g)` is
108	a sum of simple tensors with kernel entries, each of which restricts to `0` on a
109	covering sieve (local injectivity of `g`). Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`,
110	injectivity half. -/
111	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat
112	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
113	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
114	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
115	    (hg : IsLocallyInjective J g) :
116	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
117	  constructor
118	  intro X ξ η h
119	  -- View the sectionwise map of `g` as `R.obj X`-linear (the ring is commutative).
120	  let gl : ((M.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) →ₗ[(R.obj X : CommRingCat)]
121	      ((N.obj X : ModuleCat _) : Type _) := (g.app X).hom
122	  -- `h` says `F ◁ g` agrees on `ξ, η`, i.e. `lTensor` kills `ξ - η`.
123	  have hδ : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl (ξ - η) = 0 := by
124	    have heq : LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl ξ
125	        = LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl η := by
126	      rw [← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply, ← toPresheaf_whiskerLeft_app_apply]; exact h
127	    exact (map_sub (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) ξ η).trans
128	      (sub_eq_zero.mpr heq)
129	  -- Flatness: `ker(F ⊗ gl) = range(F ⊗ ker.subtype)`, so `ξ - η` is a sum of simple
130	  -- tensors with kernel entries.
131	  have hex : Function.Exact
132	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype)
133	      (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X) gl) :=
134	    Module.Flat.lTensor_exact (F.obj X) (LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map gl)
135	  obtain ⟨ζ, hζ⟩ := (hex (ξ - η)).mp hδ
136	  -- Each simple tensor `a ⊗ k` with `gl k = 0` restricts to `0` on a covering sieve
137	  -- (local injectivity of `g`); induct on the witness `ζ`.
138	  have key : ∀ ζ : TensorProduct (R.obj X) (F.obj X) (LinearMap.ker gl),
139	      Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
140	        (LinearMap.lTensor (R := (R.obj X : CommRingCat)) (F.obj X)
141	          (LinearMap.ker gl).subtype ζ) 0 ∈ J X.unop := by
142	    intro ζ
143	    induction ζ using TensorProduct.induction_on with
144	    | zero =>
145	        rw [map_zero]
146	        exact J.superset_covering (Presheaf.equalizerSieve_self_eq_top _).ge (J.top_mem _)
147	    | tmul a kk =>
148	        rw [LinearMap.lTensor_tmul]
149	        have hk : ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X kk.1
150	            = ((toPresheaf _).map g).app X (0 : ((toPresheaf _).obj M).obj X) := by
151	          rw [map_zero]
152	          erw [toPresheaf_map_app_apply]
153	          exact kk.2
154	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (hg.equalizerSieve_mem kk.1 0 hk)
155	        intro V f hf
156	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
157	        rw [map_zero] at hf ⊢
158	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe, PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul]
159	        erw [presheaf_map_apply_coe] at hf
160	        rw [Submodule.subtype_apply, hf]
161	        erw [TensorProduct.tmul_zero]; rfl
162	    | add ζ₁ ζ₂ h₁ h₂ =>
163	        rw [map_add]
164	        refine J.superset_covering ?_ (J.intersection_covering h₁ h₂)
165	        intro V f hf
166	        obtain ⟨hf1, hf2⟩ := hf
167	        rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
168	        rw [map_zero] at hf1 hf2 ⊢
169	        exact (map_add _ _ _).trans (by rw [hf1, hf2, add_zero])
170	  -- Transport `equalizerSieve (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J` to `equalizerSieve ξ η ∈ J`.
171	  have hmain : Presheaf.equalizerSieve (F := (toPresheaf _).obj (F ⊗ M)) (X := X)
172	      (ξ - η) 0 ∈ J X.unop := hζ ▸ key ζ
173	  refine J.superset_covering ?_ hmain
174	  intro V f hf
175	  rw [Presheaf.equalizerSieve_apply] at hf ⊢
176	  rw [map_zero, map_sub, sub_eq_zero] at hf
177	  exact hf
178	
179	/-- **Flat left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.**
180	(Blueprint `lem:flat_whisker_localizer`.) For a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
181	modules `F` and a morphism `g` lying in the sheafification localizer `J.W` (the
182	class of morphisms inverted by sheafification, equivalently the locally bijective
183	ones via `WEqualsLocallyBijective`), the left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` again
184	lies in `J.W`. The two halves are `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat` (where
185	flatness enters) and `isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft` (pure right-exactness).
186	This is the single non-formal ingredient of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
187	`tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the route uses no `MonoidalClosed` structure. -/
188	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
189	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
190	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
191	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
192	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
193	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
194	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hg ⊢
195	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg.1,
196	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hg.2⟩
197	
198	/-- **Flat right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
199	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for a sectionwise-flat presheaf of
200	modules `F` and a morphism `g` in the sheafification localizer `J.W`, the
201	right-whiskered morphism `g ▷ F` again lies in `J.W`. Obtained from the
202	left-whiskered statement by conjugating with the (iso) braiding of the symmetric
203	presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, using that `J.W` respects isomorphisms. -/
204	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_flat [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
205	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
206	    [∀ X, Module.Flat (R.obj X) (F.obj X)]
207	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
208	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
209	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
210	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_flat F g hg
211	  -- `g ▷ F = (β_ M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (β_ N F).inv` by braiding naturality.
212	  have hconj : g ▷ F
213	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
214	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
215	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
216	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
217	  -- `J.W` respects isos on both sides (it is the sheafification localizer).
218	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
219	  exact hwl
220	
221	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — flatness-FREE whiskering of a locally
222	bijective morphism (ROUTE (d), the live associator realization)
223	
224	The flat whiskering above (`W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_flat`) needs the SECTIONWISE
225	flatness instance `∀ U, Module.Flat (R(U)) (F(U))`, which is FALSE for invertible
226	sheaves over non-affine opens and is therefore OFF the associator critical path
227	(iter-212 finding). The associator only ever whiskers the sheafification UNIT
228	`η = toSheafify`, which is **locally bijective** (`∈ J.W`), not merely locally
229	injective. Whiskering a *locally bijective* `g` by an *arbitrary* `F` preserves
230	local bijectivity with NO flatness hypothesis: stalkwise `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x}
231	⊗_{R_x} g_x`, and since `g_x` is an isomorphism (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso on
232	the topological site, `Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW` / `TopCat.hasEnoughPoints`), the
233	tensor `id ⊗ g_x` is again an isomorphism — no flatness, because *isomorphisms*
234	tensor to isomorphisms whereas mere *injections* need flatness. This is exactly the
235	flatness-free technique Mathlib blesses for `J.W.IsMonoidal` via enough points
236	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, route (d)). -/
237	
238	section WhiskerOfW
239	
240	open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf
241	
242	variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}
243	
244	/-- **Whiskering a locally bijective morphism preserves local injectivity
245	(flatness-free).** For an *arbitrary* presheaf of modules `F` and a morphism `g`
246	whose underlying additive-presheaf map is locally bijective (`∈ J.W`), the
247	left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is locally injective.
248	
249	This is the single residual ingredient of the associator
250	`tensorObj_assoc_iso` under ROUTE (d). The mathematics is the stalkwise computation
251	`(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x`: a `J.W`-morphism on the topological site of `X`
252	is a *stalkwise isomorphism* (`TopCat.hasEnoughPoints` + the conservative-family
253	characterisation `hP.W_iff`, `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.*`, 2026), and
254	tensoring an isomorphism `g_x` by `id_{F_x}` yields an isomorphism, so `F ◁ g` is a
255	stalkwise iso, hence locally bijective, hence locally injective — for *any* `F`,
256	needing NEITHER sectionwise flatness NOR local triviality of `F`.
257	
258	The two Mathlib-side ingredients this stalkwise argument needs are
259	(d.1) the stalkwise characterisation of the module-level `J.W` on `Opens X` and
260	(d.2) the commutation `(A ⊗ᵖ B)_x ≅ A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x` of the stalk with the
261	presheaf-of-modules tensor (stalk = filtered colimit, and `tensorLeft`/`tensorRight`
262	preserve filtered colimits over a module category). Neither ships at the
263	`PresheafOfModules` level at the pinned commit; porting them is the genuine residual
264	(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, Decision 3, porting ingredients d.1/d.2). Stated
265	here with its substantive intended type so the rest of the associator closes against
266	it; see the task result for the precise missing Mathlib statements. -/
267	lemma isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
268	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
269	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
270	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
271	    IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g) := by
272	  -- ROUTE (e) residual: `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` is an iso since `g_x` is
273	  -- (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso); tensoring by `id` of an iso is flatness-free.
274	  --
275	  -- iter-214 CORRECTION (Step 0 + d.1 partial): the module-level stalk is NOT
276	  -- Mathlib-absent — `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk` supplies, for
277	  -- `X : TopCat`, `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module
278	  -- `Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` and `germ_smul`. The
279	  -- linear-stalk-map packaging (ingredient d.1) is now built project-side:
280	  -- `PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearMap` (+ `stalkLinearMap_germ`,
281	  -- `stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso`), all axiom-clean.
282	  --
283	  -- The TWO residual gaps to close this sorry (both require SPECIALISING this lemma
284	  -- to the topological site `C = Opens X` — the general-site statement here has no
285	  -- stalks; decl is UNPROTECTED so the specialisation is free, and the only consumer
286	  -- `tensorObj_assoc_iso` already works over `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`):
287	  --   (d.1-bridge) `(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W (toPresheaf-image) ↔ ∀ x,
288	  --     IsIso (stalkFunctor Ab x map)` — assemble from `HasEnoughPoints
289	  --     (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)` (`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Points.lean`) +
290	  --     `W_iff`, OR from `WEqualsLocallyBijective` + the TopCat criteria
291	  --     `locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks` /
292	  --     `app_injective_iff_stalkFunctor_map_injective`
293	  --     (needs `presheafFiber ≅ TopCat.Presheaf.stalk`, the Mathlib TODO bridge).
294	  --   (d.2) stalk ⊗ commutation `(F ⊗ᵖ M)_x ≅ F_x ⊗_{R_x} M_x` naturally identifying
295	  --     `(F ◁ g)_x` with `LinearMap.lTensor F_x (stalkLinearMap g x)` — "tensor
296	  --     commutes with the filtered colimit defining the stalk" over the varying ring;
297	  --     genuinely Mathlib-absent (largest piece). Then `stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso`
298	  --     + `LinearEquiv.lTensor` finish (flatness-free). See task result for full decomp.
299	  sorry
300	
301	/-- **Flatness-free left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
302	ROUTE (d) replacement for `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for an *arbitrary* `F` and a
303	locally bijective `g` (`∈ J.W`), the left-whiskered `F ◁ g` again lies in `J.W`.
304	Local surjectivity is free (`isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`, right-exactness);
305	local injectivity is the flatness-free stalkwise residual
306	`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. No flatness, no local triviality. -/
307	lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
308	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
309	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
310	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
311	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
312	  have hbij := hg
313	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hbij
314	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
315	  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg,
316	    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hbij.2⟩
317	
318	/-- **Flatness-free right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
319	braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_W`, mirroring `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`. -/
320	lemma W_whiskerRight_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
321	    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
322	    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
323	    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
324	    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
325	  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg
326	  have hconj : g ▷ F
327	      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
328	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
329	      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
330	  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
331	  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
332	  exact hwl
333	
334	end WhiskerOfW
335	
336	/-- **The sheafification-localization bridge.** A morphism `f` of presheaves of
337	modules whose underlying additive-presheaf map lies in the sheafification localizer
338	`J.W` is sent by the associated-sheaf-of-modules functor to an isomorphism. This is
339	the single residual of the `⊗`-invertibility associator
340	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`. It is the morphism-property
341	identity `PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms`
342	(the sheafification functor *is* the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`)
343	read at a single morphism. -/
344	lemma isIso_sheafification_map_of_W
345	    {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat} {Rsh : CategoryTheory.Sheaf J RingCat} (α : R₀ ⟶ Rsh.obj)
346	    [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
347	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat] [CategoryTheory.HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat]
348	    {A B : PresheafOfModules.{u} R₀} (f : A ⟶ B)
349	    (hf : J.W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R₀).map f)) :
350	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification α).map f) := by
351	  have h := PresheafOfModules.inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms (J := J) α
352	  have h2 : (CategoryTheory.MorphismProperty.isomorphisms (SheafOfModules Rsh)).inverseImage
353	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification α) f := by rw [← h]; exact hf
354	  exact h2
355	
356	end FlatWhisker
